channel.title: GeoPulse. Geopolitics in brief
channel.description: Signal-first briefings on geopolitics, edited by Lavkesh Dwivedi.

item.title: Before World Cup, FIFA slammed for politicising sport with Trump prize
item.description: FIFA faces criticism for awarding its Peace Prize to US President Donald Trump. Football officials and players question the decision. Football officials and players question FIFA’s decision to award its inaugural Peace Prize to US President Donald Trump.

item.title: Mount Everest summit route cleared by Sherpas after 2-week blockage
item.description: Sherpas cleared the Mount Everest summit route after a 2-week blockage. Mount Everest summit route cleared by Sherpas after 2-week blockage.

item.title: Iran says U.S. no longer in position to ‘dictate’ policy to other nations
item.description: Iran's Foreign Minister praised Russia ties and welcomed Moscow's support for diplomacy amid US tensions. U. S., Iran clash over Tehran's nuclear programme as review of atomic treaty begins at UN; Iranian Foreign Minister praises Russia ties, welcomes Moscow's support for diplomacy.

item.title: Fuel price surge from Iran war squeezes Philippine fisherfolk
item.description: Rising oil prices cut into incomes for 2 million Philippine fisherfolk as costs climb and catches fall. Rising oil prices are cutting into incomes for more than 2 million people across the country’s fishing sector, as costs climb and catches fall.

item.title: Mexican special forces arrest top commander of powerful cartel
item.description: Mexican special forces arrested Audias Flores, a top Jalisco New Generation Cartel commander, in Nayarit.

item.title: In war with Israel, Hezbollah shows it is still powerful
item.description: Hezbollah remains a significant player in the conflict with Israel, tied to Lebanon's stability and US-Iran negotiations. The group has shown its continued power despite previous setbacks. Despite setbacks, Hezbollah emerges as a key player, tied to Lebanon’s stability and US-Iran negotiations.

item.title: Father and brother of suspect get 30 years’ jail for terrorism-related offences
item.description: A father and brother of a terrorism suspect were sentenced to 30 years in jail for their roles in a May 2024 attack in Johor Bahru, Malaysia, that killed two police officers.

item.title: Alleged drug trafficking kingpin deported from Türkiye to India
item.description: Salim Dola, a 59-year-old alleged drug trafficking kingpin, was deported from Türkiye to India and handed over to the Narcotics Control Bureau. He will be produced before a court for further proceedings.

item.title: Dozens of countries urge reopening of Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Dozens of countries have called for the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz. The appeal comes amid concerns over the strategic waterway's closure. Dozens of countries urge reopening of Strait of Hormuz rudaw. net.

item.title: Trump reviews Iranian proposal aimed at reopening Strait of Hormuz
item.description: US President Trump met with his national security advisers to discuss an Iranian proposal aimed at reopening the Strait of Hormuz. The White House confirmed the meeting. The White House confirms Trump met his national security advisers on Monday to discuss the plan.

item.title: 3 Scenarios, 2 Deadlines, 1 Iran War: Donald Trump Faces Point Of No Return
item.description: Donald Trump has three scenarios for a potential war with Iran, with two deadlines looming. The US President's options include a military strike, economic sanctions, or a diplomatic solution. The consequences of inaction are unclear.

item.title: Afghan officials say Pakistani strikes killed 7, wounded 85 in first attacks since peace talks
item.description: Pakistani strikes on a university in Afghanistan killed 7 and wounded 85, according to Afghan officials. Pakistan's Ministry of Information and Broadcasting denied the reports, calling them a "blatant lie." The incident occurred despite ongoing peace talks.

item.title: South Korea's central bank cites war uncertainty in cautious approach, minutes show
item.description: South Korea's central bank cited war uncertainty in its cautious approach to monetary policy. The bank's minutes showed policymakers weighing the risks of a potential conflict with North Korea. The decision reflects the country's fragile economic situation.

item.title: Ukraine summons Israeli ambassador over ‘stolen’ grain shipments
item.description: Ukraine summoned Israel's ambassador over grain shipments it claims were stolen. The move comes as Ukrainian drones hit Russia's Tuapse oil refinery, causing a massive fire. The diplomatic spat highlights ongoing tensions between Ukraine and Russia.

item.title: Austrian goes on trial accused of plotting attack on Taylor Swift concert
item.description: A 21-year-old Austrian man went on trial for plotting an attack on Taylor Swift's concert. Prosecutors say he received training from Islamic State members on handling explosives. The trial marks a significant escalation in concerns over domestic terrorism in Austria.

item.title: The evolving China-Pakistan space cooperation
item.description: China and Pakistan are cooperating in space, reflecting their strong ties. The ‘all-weather ties’ between China and Pakistan are reflected in their space cooperation as well. The evolving China-Pakistan space cooperation.

item.title: Oil Giants Make $3,000 Every Second As US Families Struggle To Pay Bills
item.description: Oil giants are making $3,000 every second in profit while US families struggle to pay energy bills. The soaring profits of oil companies are contrasting with the financial strain on households.

item.title: China pledges to strengthen supply chains, energy security as part of 'systemic response' to
item.description: China's Politburo has pledged to strengthen supply chains and energy security as part of a systemic response to economic challenges. The economy started the year better than expected. The Politburo, a top decision-making body of the ruling Communist Party, was cited as saying by state news agency Xinhua that the economy got off to a better-than-expected start this year.

item.title: What’s happening on day 60 as diplomacy gathers pace?
item.description: The Trump team is reviewing an Iran peace plan to reopen the Strait of Hormuz. Nuclear talks may be delayed to a later stage as diplomacy gains pace. Trump team reviews Iran peace plan to reopen Hormuz with nuclear talks potentially delayed to a later stage.

item.title: Pirates Hijack Cargo Ship Near Somali Coast: UK Maritime Security
item.description: Pirates hijacked a cargo ship near the Somali coast. The 15-person crew includes two Indian nationals and 13 Syrians. The ship's 15-person crew comprises two Indian nationals and 13 Syrians.
